timeline.title: stop throwing work away
timeline.body_md: kept solving the same problems twice. the fix was always somewhere in a conversation I'd never find again. so I started writing everything down — every fix, every decision, every lesson. just notes at first. no structure, no automation. just the habit.

timeline.title: notes became infrastructure
timeline.body_md: the notes stopped being a pile of text and started being a system. structured types — sessions, fixes, decisions, lessons. search that actually works. the shift was realizing this isn't a notebook, it's the memory the whole process runs on.

timeline.title: stop jumping to code
timeline.body_md: reviewed a month of conversations and saw the same shape — diving into implementation before thinking through the approach. added a rule: state your files, state your approach, state your assumptions, then write. the pattern dropped from 47 conversations to 3 the next month.

timeline.title: one place, not twelve
timeline.body_md: rules were scattered across config files, memory files, random docs. reviewed a week of conversations and found the system following different versions of the same rule. consolidated everything into one searchable place. config files became thin pointers. no more drift.
